judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with nearly identical substance and citations; Report 1 additionally verifies claims empirically and adds more gating details (UI mismatch, stub messaging client), while Report 2 adds idempotency/dedup concerns — comparable quality overall.

report_1.evidence:
The deciding code is `auto_send_reminders_enabled(tenant)` in `backend/app/feature_flags.py`, lines 11–12:
```python
11    if tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders") is None:
12        return True
```

report_2.evidence:
The deciding code is `auto_send_reminders_enabled` in `backend/app/feature_flags.py`, lines 11–12:

```python
if tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders") is None:
    return True
```

report_1.evidence: No — it is the wrong default and should be inverted... Change it at `backend/app/feature_flags.py:10–13`. The minimal fix is to drop the `is None → return True` special case

report_2.evidence: No — for this feature the default should be **off (opt-in)**, and I would change it... Where I'd change it — `backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-13`: drop the `is None → return True` special case so an unset value is falsy

report_1.evidence: `25509a7` (SLOT-190, 2026-06-10) created `feature_flags.py` containing only `compact_calendar_view_enabled`, whose missing→`True` (grandfather-on) default is appropriate for a UI/calendar preference... `aa4cf8d` (SLOT-214, 2026-07-01) added `auto_send_reminders_enabled` as a **byte-for-byte structural clone** of that function... the two flags sit in opposite categories (UI convenience vs. outbound messaging).

report_2.evidence: SLOT-190 (`25509a7`) created `feature_flags.py` with `compact_calendar_view_enabled`, which defaults unset→`True`. That is a **cosmetic calendar display preference**... SLOT-214 (`aa4cf8d`) added `auto_send_reminders_enabled` as a **verbatim structural clone** of that function... the two flags have opposite risk profiles.

report_1.evidence: There is currently **no global gate** — `run()` (`reminders.py:22–31`) consults only the per-tenant resolver (confirmed by grep: no master/global switch anywhere; `infra/main.tf` holds only `environment`, default `"staging"`). A safe rollout needs a single kill-switch/rollback lever and per-environment staging

report_2.evidence: The per-tenant flag at `reminders.py:25` is the *only* thing between "enabled" and "every clinic messages every patient." `infra/main.tf` only defines an `environment` variable (default `"staging"`) — there is no global feature flag, no per-environment guard, no dry-run/canary... I'd add a global enable plus a canary/allowlist rollout
